what: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: “Warp speed!” cptkirk warps remote Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs fast: saturates network concurrent reads, hands warp GDAL. combines two tools best--class one thing: async-tiff saturates remote byte-range reads (Cloud-Optimised) GeoTIFFs decodes tiles concurrently. GDAL (via gdalraster) best warper . cptkirk thin pipe . gdalwarp-style request works exactly source pixels warp need, streams tiles async-tiff appropriate overview level, stages -memory GDAL source, hands actual reprojection resampling GDAL. reimplements none GDAL’s warp logic; sizes saturates fetch.

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: ck_warp() recommended, batteries-included entry point, named t_srs / te / tr / ts / r / bands arguments plus sensible defaults (multi-threaded warp, generous working memory, tr-grid-aligned output tiles resolution stack cleanly). warp_remote() faithful, defaults-free sibling gdalraster::warp(), src / dst / t_srs / cl_arg call shape source streamed remotely. Reach want GDAL’s exact behaviour full control via cl_arg. validate request tiny -memory probe fetching, bad CRS, resampling method creation option fails milliseconds instead download.

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
code: # requires a Rust toolchain (rustc >= 1.78) and GDAL (via gdalraster) pak::pak("belian-earth/cptkirk")

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: t_srs / te / tr / ts / r follow gdalwarp interface, extra raw flags pass straight via cl_arg. ck_warp() aligns output tr grid default (gdalwarp -tap); set tap = FALSE, use warp_remote(), GDAL’s exact requested extent. Warping many areas raster? Open cog_source() pass handle place URL pay metadata round-trips .
code: library(cptkirk)  # inspect a remote COG (header + IFDs only, no pixels fetched) url <- paste0(   "https://data.source.coop/tge-labs/aef/v1/annual/2021/36S/",   "xekh5rjs4wg6wb9b4-0000000000-0000000000.tiff" )  (m <- cog_info(url)) #> ── cog_info ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> <https://data.source.coop/tge-labs/aef/v1/annual/2021/36S/xekh5rjs4wg6wb9b4-0000000000-0000000000.tiff> #> size: 8192 x 8192 px (67.1 Mpx) #> bands: 64 Int8 #> resolution: 10 (CRS units) #> crs: EPSG:32736 #> nodata: -128 #> overviews: 13 (8192x8192, 4096x4096, 2048x2048, 1024x1024, 512x512, 256x256, #> 128x128, 64x64, 32x32, 16x16, 8x8, 4x4, 2x2, 1x1) #> band names: "A00", "A01", "A02", "A03", "A04", "A05", …, "A62", and "A63"  # warp just a ~6 km window of the tile -- only the source tiles covering this # extent are streamed gt <- m$geotransform x0 <- gt[1] + 4000 * gt[2] y0 <- gt[4] + 4000 * gt[6] te <- c(x0, y0 - 6000, x0 + 6000, y0)   # AOI in the tile's CRS (EPSG:32736)  r <- ck_warp(   src   = url,   dst   = tempfile(fileext = ".tif"),   te    = te,                # area of interest   tr    = c(30, 30),         # 30 m output (an overview is selected)   r     = "average",   bands = c(1, 2, 3)         # subset bands at the fetch )  ds <- new(gdalraster::GDALRaster, r) gdalraster::plot_raster(ds, xsize = 800, ysize = 800) ds$close()

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: Remote object-store sources (s3://, gs://, az://) authenticate process environment, using variable names object_store documents (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID / AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY / AWS_REGION, GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT, AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME, …). Credentials never pass R. public buckets set AWS_SKIP_SIGNATURE=true, just use https:// URL.

what: How it picks what to fetch
title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: Window. target extent reprojected source CRS (edge densification) find source-pixel window output covers. Overview. target resolution mapped source units pick finest overview level whose decimation exceed output needs. Bands. bands = subsets fetch, requested bands’ bytes streamed. streamed window becomes -memory GDAL dataset; GDAL rest.

what: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG with cptkirk's defaults (recommended) — ck_warp
title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG with cptkirk's defaults (recommended) — ck_warp
text: recommended, batteries-included entry point. ck_warp() takes grid-defining gdalwarp options named arguments (t_srs, te, tr, ts, r, bands), layers cptkirk's performance defaults (multi-threaded warp, generous warp memory block cache, SKIP_NOSOURCE), streams pixels request touches async-tiff handing warp GDAL via gdalraster::warp(). faithful, defaults-free sibling gdalraster::warp(), see warp_remote().

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG with cptkirk's defaults (recommended) — ck_warp
code: ck_warp(   src,   dst,   t_srs = NULL,   te = NULL,   te_srs = NULL,   tr = NULL,   ts = NULL,   tap = TRUE,   r = c("near", "bilinear", "cubic", "cubicspline", "lanczos", "average", "rms", "mode",     "max", "min", "med", "q1", "q3", "sum"),   bands = NULL,   cl_arg = character(0),   num_threads = "ALL_CPUS",   warp_memory = "auto",   cache_max = "auto",   co = c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE", "TILED=YES", "NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS", "BIGTIFF=IF_SAFER"),   config = NULL,   skip_nosource = TRUE,   overview = NULL,   margin = 8L,   io_concurrency = 16L,   max_bytes = NULL,   sanitise = TRUE )

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG with cptkirk's defaults (recommended) — ck_warp
text: src Path/URL source GeoTIFF / COG, cog_source() handle, character vector several sources mosaic (tile's overlapping window fetched set reprojected/mosaicked one warp; non-overlapping tiles skipped). Passing cog_source() reuses already-open handle, skipping metadata re-read – worthwhile warping many AOIs source. dst Output filename (regular path, /vsimem/ path, anything GDAL can write). format inferred GDAL set -cl_arg. t_srs Target SRS (e.g. "EPSG:3857"). NULL, source CRS used (reprojection). te Target extent c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax), te_srs given, otherwise t_srs. supplied AOI drives fetch. te_srs SRS te (defaults t_srs). tr Target resolution c(xres, yres) target CRS units. Takes precedence ts: given, ts ignored (warning). ts Target size c(width, height) pixels. Ignored tr also supplied. tap TRUE (default) tr given, align output pixel boundaries tr grid (gdalwarp -tap), anchored CRS origin, outputs resolution share grid stack cleanly. snaps output extent outward, longer exactly te. effect ts copy path. explicit -tap cl_arg always honoured. (warp_remote() never adds ; pass -tap .) r Resampling method, one "near" (default), "bilinear", "cubic", "cubicspline", "lanczos", "average", "rms", "mode", "max", "min", "med", "q1", "q3", "sum". Matched rlang::arg_match(), typo reports valid set. (method added newer GDAL list knows can still passed via cl_arg.) bands 1-based source bands read (default: ). Subsetting happens fetch, bands' bytes streamed. cl_arg Character vector extra raw gdalwarp flags, forwarded verbatim gdalraster::warp() (e.g. c("-et", "0")). merged flags cptkirk builds named arguments . num_threads Value GDAL's warp NUM_THREADS warp option GDAL_NUM_THREADS config (default "ALL_CPUS"), parallelising resampling computation GeoTIFF (de)compression. NULL sets neither, deferring ambient GDAL_NUM_THREADS (env / session). warp_memory Warp working memory MB (GDAL -wm). "auto" (default) uses ~25% system RAM, clamped 256-2048 MB (bigger means fewer, larger chunks); NULL defers GDAL's default; number sets explicitly. cache_max GDAL block cache MB call. "auto" (default) uses ~25% RAM, clamped 256-2000 MB; NULL defers ambient GDAL_CACHEMAX (env / gdalraster::set_config_option()); number sets explicitly. value restored previous setting afterwards. co GDAL output creation options, defaulting cloud-friendly GeoTIFF set: c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE", "TILED=YES", "NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS", "BIGTIFF=IF_SAFER") — losslessly compressed, tiled (output can re-read cptkirk COG reader), compressed parallel, promoted BigTIFF might exceed 4 GB. Pass override (e.g. add "PREDICTOR=2" integer data, "COMPRESS=ZSTD" GDAL built ), NULL creation options. target GTiff/COG drivers; set co output formats. config Named character vector / list extra GDAL config options set duration call (restored exit). skip_nosource TRUE (default), pass SKIP_NOSOURCE=YES + INIT_DEST warper skips output chunks source coverage (e.g. nodata margins reprojection). effect output fully covered. Ignored copy fast-path. overview Force 1-based IFD/overview level instead auto-selecting output resolution. 1 = full resolution. margin Source-pixel margin added around computed window cover resampling kernel reprojection slop (default 8). io_concurrency Number concurrent tile reads – width single global fetch pool shared across source tiles. Default 16, suits object stores throttle around many simultaneous range requests (e.g. S3 / source.coop). Raise (24-32) fast, stable link; lower store rate-limits. max_bytes Safety ceiling (bytes) staged -memory window (width * height * bands * <native dtype bytes>). NULL (default) uses ~1/3 system RAM. guards foot-gun warping whole large multi-band raster native resolution; narrow request, coarsen tr/ts, raise allow . sanitise TRUE (default), validate warp arguments tiny metadata-derived stand-fetching, bad CRS, resampling method, creation option unknown flag fails milliseconds instead remote read. Set FALSE skip check.

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG with cptkirk's defaults (recommended) — ck_warp
text: dst path, invisibly.

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG with cptkirk's defaults (recommended) — ck_warp
text: Every actual resampling / reprojection decision GDAL's; cptkirk sizes saturates fetch.

title: Inspect a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG — cog_info
text: Reads GeoTIFF header IFDs remote-capable reader (local path, http(s)://, s3://, gs://, az://) returns georeferencing structure. pixel data fetched.

title: Inspect a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG — cog_info
Inspect a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG — cog_info
text: src Path URL GeoTIFF / Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFF, cog_source() handle.

title: Inspect a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG — cog_info
text: list (class cog_info) width, height, n_bands, dtype (GDAL type name), nodata, geotransform (GDAL corner-based affine, length 6), crs (string GDAL can import), band_names, per-level pixel sizes level_width / level_height (level 1 = full resolution, rest overviews) plus tile_width / tile_height.

what: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
title: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
text: Opens source remote-capable reader (local path, http(s)://, s3://, gs://, az://), reading header IFDs single time. returned handle can passed cog_info() warp_remote() place URL, repeated warps raster (e.g. many AOIs, set rasters) pay metadata round-trips .

title: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
text: src Path URL GeoTIFF / Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFF.

title: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
text: object class cog_source wrapping open handle.

title: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
text: Remote object-store sources (s3://, gs://, az://) authenticate process environment, using variable names object_store documents (e.g. AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SESSION_TOKEN, AWS_REGION; GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT; AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME). static credentials builders fall back platform chain (web-identity, ECS, EC2 instance metadata). public buckets set AWS_SKIP_SIGNATURE=true (just use https:// URL). Credentials never passed R, stay scripts logs. independent GDAL's /vsi* credential settings, cptkirk use reading.

what: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: remote, overview-aware gdalwarp. cptkirk works source pixels requested warp touch, streams tiles Rust async-tiff reader (high remote-read saturation, appropriate overview level), stages -memory GDAL source, hands actual reprojection resampling GDAL via gdalraster — following gdalwarp argument interface.

title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: Two entry points onto engine: ck_warp() — recommended, batteries-included helper: named t_srs / te / tr / ts / r / bands arguments plus cptkirk's performance defaults (multi-threading, generous warp memory cache, SKIP_NOSOURCE, band-subset streaming). warp_remote() — faithful, defaults-free sibling gdalraster::warp(): src / dst / t_srs / cl_arg call shape, source streamed remotely. Forwards cl_arg GDAL verbatim.

title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: cog_info() — read source's structure georeferencing (header IFDs ; pixels fetched) cog_source() — open source reuse handle across calls

title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: gdalwarp-style request cptkirk: reprojects target extent source's CRS find pixel window source must supply; selects overview level matching output resolution; streams just tiles concurrently async-tiff (one global concurrency pool across tiles); stages /vsimem lets GDAL perform warp, mosaicking multiple source tiles single pass. reimplements none GDAL's warp logic; sizes saturates fetch. Sources may local paths http(s)://, s3://, gs://, az:// URLs.

title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: Maintainer: Hugh Graham hugh@belian.earth Authors: Hugh Graham hugh@belian.earth

what: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
text: near drop-gdalraster::warp() reads source(s) remotely. call shape – src, dst, t_srs, raw gdalwarp argument vector cl_arg – instead letting GDAL pull source /vsicurl, cptkirk works pixels request touches streams just tiles Rust async-tiff reader (appropriate overview level), stages /vsimem, hands warp GDAL. Output identical gdalraster::warp() source.

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
code: warp_remote(   src,   dst,   t_srs = "",   cl_arg = NULL,   quiet = TRUE,   overview = NULL,   margin = 8L,   io_concurrency = 16L,   max_bytes = NULL,   sanitise = TRUE )

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
text: src Path/URL source GeoTIFF / COG, cog_source() handle, character vector several sources mosaic. cog_source() reuses already-open handle, skipping metadata re-read. dst Output filename (regular path, /vsimem/, anything GDAL can write). format inferred GDAL set -cl_arg. t_srs Target SRS (e.g. "EPSG:3857"), gdalraster::warp(). "" (default) NULL means reprojection (source CRS). cl_arg Character vector raw gdalwarp flags, forwarded verbatim gdalraster::warp() (e.g. c("-te", "0", "0", "100", "100", "-tr", "10", "10", "-r", "bilinear")). cptkirk parses -te/-te_srs/-tr/-ts purely size fetch. quiet TRUE (default), GDAL warp runs without progress callback. Unlike gdalraster::warp() defaults TRUE: progress callback invoked GDAL's worker threads (e.g. -multi) can crash R session. overview Force 1-based IFD/overview level instead auto-selecting output resolution. 1 = full resolution. margin Source-pixel margin added around computed window cover resampling kernel reprojection slop (default 8). io_concurrency Number concurrent tile reads – width single global fetch pool shared across source tiles (default 16). max_bytes Safety ceiling (bytes) staged -memory window. NULL (default) uses ~1/3 system RAM. sanitise TRUE (default), validate cl_arg (t_srs) tiny metadata-derived stand-fetching, bad CRS, resampling method, creation option unknown flag fails milliseconds instead remote read. Set FALSE skip check.

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
text: dst path, invisibly.

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
text: faithful low-level interface: adds none cptkirk's performance opinions forwards cl_arg GDAL verbatim. recommended, batteries-included helper (named te/tr/ts/r/bands arguments, multi-threading, SKIP_NOSOURCE, band-subset streaming, ...) use ck_warp().
